body:
### User Story
As a user of the Windows 95 desktop simulation, I want a more authentic and functional Media Player experience so that it feels closer to the original OS and allows me to easily play all available sound effects.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Redesign UI**: UI closely matches the Windows 95 Media Player (`mplayer.exe`) with a compact layout.
- [ ] **Reduce Spacing**: Tighten spacing and padding to remove modern gaps.
- [ ] **Authentic Icons**: Replace current icons with versions that match Windows 95 style (Play, Pause, Stop, Skip).
- [ ] **Improved Timer**: Replace the current duration text with a standard `0:00 / 0:30` (or similar) format.
- [ ] **Integrated Playlist**: Show a list of all available sound effects in `public/sfx` with titles derived from filenames.
- [ ] **Functional Parity**: Maintain volume control and playback logic.

### Technical Notes
- Modify `components/win95/MusicPlayer.tsx`.
- Load tracks dynamically from `public/sfx` and `soundConfig.json`.
- Use existing Win95 CSS classes but refine internal spacing.
title: [Feature]: Improved Windows 95 Media Player Experience
